vitals.location: Bento Gonçalves, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil
vitals.date: Last held: May 4, 2025 — no 2026 edition confirmed
vitals.date_specific: Last held: May 4, 2025 — no 2026 edition confirmed
vitals.field_size: approximately 800 (2022-2023 editions; no current field/capacity published)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66. WAVE 6 (2026-07-24): distance 4->3 to match the verified last-held (2025) course of 122.5km, which falls in the 120-160km rubric band rather than 160-200km; the prior 170.6km figure was unsupported by any documented edition (real course history: 2022 ~145km, 2023 135.9km, 2025 122.5km — the route has been redesigned repeatedly, not stable at 170.6km). climbing left at 4 (3,218m verified 2025 elevation still falls in the 2,500-4,000m rubric). road_surface left at 1 (predominantly paved; historic cobbled sectors documented only on the discontinued 2022-23 route, not confirmed on the 2025 course). overall_score recalculated 66->64 per formula; tier remains T2.

course_description.character: The event used Fundaparque, Bento Gonçalves' expo center, for race-week operations and the start/finish. The competitive distance changed from about 145 km in 2022 to 135.9 km in 2023, followed by a materially redesigned 122.5 km route in 2025 — limiting year-to-year comparability rather than offering one stable, repeatable course. The original 2022-23 alignment started/finished at Fundaparque with a 75 km loop east and south (an early 10 km gradual climb with 8-10% sections, then a 3 km climb into Caravaggio), followed by 40 km of rolling countryside through Farroupilha, Carlos Barbosa and Garibaldi, before the medium-course finish and a long-course-only 60 km western extension through the Vale dos Vinhedos wine region — vineyard-covered hills, historic cobbled sections, repeated 1-4 km climbs at 8-16%, and a 12 km/~4% closing climb out of Santa Tereza. For the 2025 edition (the last held, and the one this profile's vitals now reflect), the route was redesigned: Igreja Pipa, Caminhos de Pedra, Ponte de Ferro, Nova Roma do Sul, Casa Corba, Pinto Bandeira, and the Barracão climb back toward Alameda Fenavinho and Fundaparque — still Serra Gaúcha wine country, but a different course than the earlier Vale dos Vinhedos loop. No 2026 edition has been confirmed as of this review.
course_description.signature_challenge: On the 2022-23 course, the Vale dos Vinhedos western extension's 12 km/~4% closing climb out of Santa Tereza, arriving after a full day of shorter 8-16% ramps. On the redesigned 2025 course, the Barracão climb late in the return toward Fundaparque.
suffering_zones: The early climbing block out of Fundaparque (2022-23 course) — an immediate 10 km gradual climb with 8-10% sections right from the start
The Vale dos Vinhedos loop's repeated short, steep ramps (1-4 km at 8-16%) — the 2022-23 course's defining western extension
The redesigned 2025 route's Barracão climb and the closing stretch back toward Alameda Fenavinho and Fundaparque

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels in Bento Gonçalves; ExpoBento/Fundaparque hosts race week activities.

history.origin_story: Founded 2022 as GFNY's return to Brazil, held October 16 that year, distinct from the older GFNY Conservatória chapter in Rio de Janeiro state. Ran three editions total: 2022, 2023, and a May 4, 2025 edition (the planned October 2024 third edition was postponed after the catastrophic Rio Grande do Sul floods).

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY Bento Gonçalves is a GFNY World Series race with a three-edition history (2022, 2023, and a flood-delayed 2025) in Brazil's Serra Gaúcha wine region, with a ~800-rider field in its first two years. But it is currently absent from GFNY's live global calendar: the official Bento site is reduced to a 'Subscribe to learn more' placeholder still showing 2025 content, the registration page has no date or price, and the results archive stops at 2025. A Brazil-specific site operated locally by Global Sports advertised May 3, 2026, but that edition never appeared in GFNY's global calendar or official results archive — the regional homepage still displays the stale listing, while its linked event-detail page now redirects to an account-suspended page. No organizer statement formally discontinuing the race was found, so this is an evidence-based inactivity call, not a confirmed shutdown.
strengths: Three-edition history (2022, 2023, 2025) as part of the legitimate GFNY World Series, distinct from GFNY's other Brazil chapter (Conservatória)
~800-rider field in its first two years, run through Serra Gaúcha wine country with 3,218 m of climbing on the 2025 long course
The flood-postponed 2024 edition was eventually held on May 4, 2025, rather than being cancelled outright
weaknesses: No confirmed edition since May 2025 — GFNY's own global race finder omits Bento Gonçalves through its published 2027 calendar
The competitive distance changed from about 145 km in 2022 to 135.9 km in 2023, followed by a materially redesigned 122.5 km route in 2025, limiting year-to-year comparability

A Brazil-specific regional site advertised a May 3, 2026 date that never produced a result in GFNY's official archive or the reviewed regional pages, and whose event-detail link now redirects to an account-suspended page — a confusing, unresolved status for anyone trying to plan around this race
No current fee, field cap, or working registration process appears on the official Bento pages reviewed
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A race with a genuine three-edition history, but there's nothing to register for right now. Don't plan travel around a future edition without direct confirmation from GFNY's official channels first.

final_verdict.one_liner: A genuine but currently dormant GFNY Brazil chapter — three editions through 2025, nothing confirmed since.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Not right now — no confirmed future edition exists, and the event is absent from GFNY's live global calendar. Verify directly with GFNY before planning travel.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an active GFNY Brazil race, check GFNY Conservatória (Rio de Janeiro state, returned 2024) directly via gfny.com/race-finder.

youtube_data.search_query: GFNY Bento Gonçalves gran fondo cycling Bento Gonçalves

videos.title: GFNY BENTO Gonçalves - Brazil - Gran Fondo New York
videos.description:
Be a Pro For a Day 
______
Bento Gonçalves has the largest and most equipped expo center in Rio Grande do Sul, the Fundaparque, which hosts international events like Movelsul, Fimma, Fiema, ExpoBento, and is also your host for GFNY Bento Goncalves race week activities. Get ready for fun activities like photo walls, beer garden, group rides, information sessions, music and the pre-race expo.

The race starts and finishes next to the Fundaparque and makes a 75km loop to the east and south. You’ll start with a 3km descent and then start 10km of climbing, which is a gradual climb, but also has steep sections of 8-10%. You’ll descend 3km again, and then have a 3km climb into the village of Caravaggio.

From Caravaggio, you will ride along a 40km stretch of rolling hills, which will be quick and fun with your peloton. You’ll be traveling on tranquil countryside roads along this loop, but also pass through cities like Farroupilha, Carlos Barbosa and Garibaldi. After Garibaldi, the rolling hills continue, but they become longer and stepper, 2-4km climbs followed by similar descents. Here is where the groups will start to splinter.

As the loop comes to an end, you will have to make your decision to continue cycling the long course by turning left for a loop through the stunning Vale dos Vinhedos or head to the finish line of the medium route.

The loop to the west is is 60km and travels through the heart of the Bento Goncalves wine region, with vineyard-covered hills, Mediterranean-style buildings, small shops, historic cobble sections and breathtaking scenery. The loop covers the entire length of the famous Vale dos Vinhedos. But with great beauty also comes great challenge. The loop has many steep climbs of 8-16% which are 1-4km long. When you reach the western-most part of the loop, you’ll navigate a steep descent into Santa Tereza and then take on the longest climb of the day: 12km and 4% average. A few more steep climbs will await you to close this challenging loop.

Once you complete the loop, it’s a fast, flat final 5km to the finish line where the day started. Come to Fundaparque to receive your medal, post-race meal and celebrate your finish and the special achievement with your cycling group, friends and family

#VEMPRABENTO

videos.title: GFNY Bento Gonçalves
videos.description:
Registro do maior e mais épico evento de 🚴 ciclismo que tivemos a honra de participar, o Gran Fondo NY - Bento Gonçalves

A GFNY é a marca esportiva global de enduro. A GFNY oferece um conjunto consistente de regras, um pelotão internacional e corridas em destinos que oferecem uma grande experiência para atletas e famílias. Na GFNY, você tem a oportunidade de correr o seu melhor, testar sua força física e mental, superar barreiras e alcançar a linha de chegada. 🏆

🇧🇷 A edição brasileira, sediada em Bento Gonçalves, percorreu 140km em seu percurso longo, passando pelas mais belas paisagens e cidades da região, incluindo #farroupilha, #montebelodosul, #garibaldi, #santatereza e mais. 

As inscrições para a edição 2023 já estão abertas, e podem ser feitas em bento.gfny.com ✅

Aqui, o vídeo oficial promocional, que mostra apenas um pedacinho do que foi a experiência de quem participou, seja como atleta, expositor, família ou visitante.

#drone #FPV #fpvdrone #filmagem #aérea #protek25 #teamiflight #bentogoncalves #gfnybento #ciclismo #gfny #ciclismobrasil

youtube_data.course_note: This video's course description (75km loop, Vale dos Vinhedos western extension) reflects the original 2022-23 route, not the redesigned 2025 course this profile's vitals now describe. Retained as genuine event footage, not purged, since it is real GFNY Bento Gonçalves content — just from an earlier course.

catalog_flags.status_note: Corrected during wave-6 sol review (2026-07-24): eligibility.status changed from a first-draft 'defunct' to 'unknown' — this repo's own scripts/eligibility_audit.py defines defunct as organizer-confirmed ending OR 2+ years with no edition and no announcement; Bento last ran May 2025, only ~14 months before this review, well short of that threshold (contrast the GFNY Philippines precedent, which had 6+ years of silence before being marked defunct). The editorial verdict 'Inactive — No Edition Since 2025' is retained since it accurately describes the current gap without overstating it via the machine-readable status. Flagging for a human call in case GFNY reactivates this chapter, an official closure statement surfaces, or enough time passes to justify 'defunct' under the repo's own rule.

eligibility.notes: INACTIVE, NOT YET DEFUNCT UNDER THIS REPO'S OWN RULE (2026-07-24, wave 6): GFNY Bento Gonçalves ran three editions — 2022, 2023, and a flood-delayed May 4, 2025 — but is absent from GFNY's current global race finder through its published 2027 calendar. The official Bento site is reduced to a 'Subscribe to learn more' placeholder still showing 2025 content; its registration page has no date or price; its results archive stops at 2025 with no 2026 entry. A Brazil-specific site operated locally by Global Sports (gfnybrazil.com.br, distinct from GFNY's global site) advertised May 3, 2026, but that edition never appeared in GFNY's global calendar or official results archive — the regional homepage still shows the stale listing, while its linked event-detail page now redirects to an account-suspended page. No organizer statement formally discontinuing the race was found. Status is 'unknown' rather than 'defunct': scripts/eligibility_audit.py's own rule requires organizer confirmation OR 2+ years of silence for a defunct call, and Bento's ~14-month gap since May 2025 falls well short of that (contrast GFNY Philippines, wave 5, which had 6+ years before being marked defunct).
